summary:
Postcoital bleeding combined with a palpably hemorrhagic mass suggests a cervical polyp. Cervical polyps are known for easy bleeding upon contact and may show slight cellular changes due to potential inflammation; these features match the clinical presentation. Although uterine fibroids can cause similar symptoms, their internal position is less likely to result in immediate post-coital bleeding compared to an externally located polyp.
## Final Answer: D

---
